name: Donacobiidae
description: Donacobiidae is a monotypic family of passerine bird found in wetlands of South America. The single species is noted for its loud, duetted songs and its large, deep cup nests built over water.

children.name: Donacobius
children.description: A monotypic genus of marsh-dwelling passerine found in South American wetlands.

children.name: Donacobius atricapilla
children.description: The Black-capped Donacobius is a striking black, white, and chestnut bird with a long, rounded tail. It inhabits reed beds and marsh edges from Panama to Argentina, singing loud, synchronized duets with its mate.
